tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria, suggesting possible diabetic nephropathy or another renal pathology despite low HbA1c. Urine is cloudy, brown, and has low specific gravity, further supporting renal dysfunction. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying causes.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
